psalm-68-1.verse: Let God arise, let His enemies be scattered, And let those who hate Him flee before Him.
psalm-68-1.prayer: Lord, rise up in power and scatter everything that opposes Your truth, Your goodness, and Your will in my life.

psalm-68-2.verse: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away; As wax melts before the fire, So let the wicked perish before God.
psalm-68-2.prayer: Father, remove what is evil, false, and harmful. Let Your righteousness prevail and purify my heart before You.

psalm-68-3.verse: But let the righteous be glad; let them exult before God; Yes, let them rejoice with gladness.
psalm-68-3.prayer: Lord, help me rejoice in Your presence. Fill my heart with gladness because You are faithful, righteous, and good.

psalm-68-4.verse: Sing to God, sing praises to His name; Lift up a song for Him who rides through the deserts, Whose name is the Lord, and exult before Him.
psalm-68-4.prayer: Father, teach me to sing praises to Your name. Help me worship You with joy and honor You as the One who reigns above all.

psalm-68-5.verse: A father of the fatherless and a judge for the widows, Is God in His holy habitation.
psalm-68-5.prayer: Lord, thank You for being a Father to the fatherless and a defender of the vulnerable. Help me reflect Your compassion and care.

psalm-68-6.verse: God makes a home for the lonely; He leads out the prisoners into prosperity, Only the rebellious dwell in a parched land.
psalm-68-6.prayer: Father, thank You for placing the lonely in families and bringing freedom to those who are bound. Use my life to bring encouragement and hope.

psalm-68-7.verse: O God, when You went forth before Your people, When You marched through the wilderness, Selah.
psalm-68-7.prayer: Lord, thank You for leading Your people through every wilderness. Lead me faithfully through every season of uncertainty.

psalm-68-8.verse: The earth quaked; The heavens also dropped rain at the presence of God; Sinai itself quaked at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
psalm-68-8.prayer: Father, creation trembles before Your presence. Help me live with reverence, awe, and confidence in Your power.

psalm-68-9.verse: You shed abroad a plentiful rain, O God; You confirmed Your inheritance when it was parched.
psalm-68-9.prayer: Lord, thank You for sending abundant rain and refreshing what is weary. Restore my soul when I feel dry or weak.

psalm-68-10.verse: Your creatures settled in it; You provided in Your goodness for the poor, O God.
psalm-68-10.prayer: Father, thank You for providing for Your people. Help me trust Your care and recognize Your goodness in every blessing.

psalm-68-11.verse: The Lord gives the command; The women who proclaim the good tidings are a great host:
psalm-68-11.prayer: Lord, let Your Word go forth with power. Help me receive Your truth and share Your goodness with faithfulness.

psalm-68-12.verse: “Kings of armies flee, they flee, And she who remains at home will divide the spoil!”
psalm-68-12.prayer: Father, thank You for giving victory that does not depend on human strength. Help me trust in Your power and provision.

psalm-68-13.verse: When you lie down among the sheepfolds, You are like the wings of a dove covered with silver, And its pinions with glistening gold.
psalm-68-13.prayer: Lord, even in ordinary places, You reveal beauty and blessing. Help me see Your grace in every part of my life.

psalm-68-14.verse: When the Almighty scattered the kings there, It was snowing in Zalmon.
psalm-68-14.prayer: Father, You scatter darkness and bring light. Let Your victory shine over every place of fear, confusion, or discouragement.

psalm-68-15.verse: A mountain of God is the mountain of Bashan; A mountain of many peaks is the mountain of Bashan.
psalm-68-15.prayer: Lord, Your mountain is majestic and strong. Help me remember that Your kingdom is greater than every earthly power.

psalm-68-16.verse: Why do you look with envy, O mountains with many peaks, At the mountain which God has desired for His abode? Surely the Lord will dwell there forever.
psalm-68-16.prayer: Father, thank You for choosing to dwell with Your people. Make my heart a place where Your presence is welcomed and honored.

psalm-68-17.verse: The chariots of God are myriads, thousands upon thousands; The Lord is among them as at Sinai, in holiness.
psalm-68-17.prayer: Lord, You are surrounded by heavenly power and glory. Help me trust that You are never lacking strength, authority, or help.

psalm-68-18.verse: You have ascended on high, You have led captive Your captives; You have received gifts among men, Even among the rebellious also, that the Lord God may dwell there.
psalm-68-18.prayer: Father, thank You for the victory and gifts You give. Help me use every blessing to honor You and serve others.

psalm-68-19.verse: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burden, The God who is our salvation. Selah.
psalm-68-19.prayer: Lord, blessed be Your name. Thank You for daily bearing my burdens and sustaining me with Your grace.

psalm-68-20.verse: God is to us a God of deliverances; And to God the Lord belong escapes from death.
psalm-68-20.prayer: Father, You are the God of salvation and deliverance. Help me trust You with my life, my future, and every burden I carry.

psalm-68-21.verse: Surely God will shatter the head of His enemies, The hairy crown of him who goes on in his guilty deeds.
psalm-68-21.prayer: Lord, defeat the power of sin and anything that rebels against Your truth in my heart. Lead me in righteousness.

psalm-68-22.verse: The Lord said, “I will bring them back from Bashan. I will bring them back from the depths of the sea;
psalm-68-22.prayer: Father, no place is beyond Your reach. Thank You that You can rescue, restore, and bring back what seems lost.

psalm-68-23.verse: That your foot may shatter them in blood, The tongue of your dogs may have its portion from your enemies.”
psalm-68-23.prayer: Lord, establish Your justice and protect Your people. Help me trust Your righteous judgment in all things.

psalm-68-24.verse: They have seen Your procession, O God, The procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
psalm-68-24.prayer: Father, help me recognize Your procession and movement in my life. Let me follow where You lead with worship and faith.

psalm-68-25.verse: The singers went on, the musicians after them, In the midst of the maidens beating tambourines.
psalm-68-25.prayer: Lord, let my life be filled with praise, worship, and joyful gratitude before You.

psalm-68-26.verse: Bless God in the congregations, Even the Lord, you who are of the fountain of Israel.
psalm-68-26.prayer: Father, help me bless You among Your people and honor You with a sincere and faithful heart.

psalm-68-27.verse: There is Benjamin, the youngest, ruling them, The princes of Judah in their throng, The princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
psalm-68-27.prayer: Lord, thank You for calling every person and every generation into Your purposes. Help me serve faithfully where You place me.

psalm-68-28.verse: Your God has commanded your strength; Show Yourself strong, O God, who have acted on our behalf.
psalm-68-28.prayer: Father, strengthen what You have worked in me. Confirm Your purposes and help me walk in Your power.

psalm-68-29.verse: Because of Your temple at Jerusalem Kings will bring gifts to You.
psalm-68-29.prayer: Lord, may my life bring offerings of praise and devotion to You, the King who is worthy of all honor.

psalm-68-30.verse: Rebuke the beasts in the reeds, The herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples, Trampling under foot the pieces of silver; He has scattered the peoples who delight in war.
psalm-68-30.prayer: Father, restrain evil and humble every proud power. Let peace, justice, and righteousness increase.

psalm-68-31.verse: Envoys will come out of Egypt; Ethiopia will quickly stretch out her hands to God.
psalm-68-31.prayer: Lord, draw people from every nation toward You. Let hearts everywhere lift their hands in worship and surrender.

psalm-68-32.verse: Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth, Sing praises to the Lord, Selah.
psalm-68-32.prayer: Father, may all kingdoms of the earth sing praises to You. Help my life join that song with joy and faithfulness.

psalm-68-33.verse: To Him who rides upon the highest heavens, which are from ancient times; Behold, He speaks forth with His voice, a mighty voice.
psalm-68-33.prayer: Lord, You ride upon the highest heavens and speak with mighty power. Help me listen with reverence and trust.

psalm-68-34.verse: Ascribe strength to God; His majesty is over Israel And His strength is in the skies.
psalm-68-34.prayer: Father, all strength belongs to You. Help me depend on Your power and give You glory for every victory.

psalm-68-35.verse: O God, You are awesome from Your sanctuary. The God of Israel Himself gives strength and power to the people. Blessed be God!
psalm-68-35.prayer: Lord, You are awesome from Your sanctuary and You give strength to Your people. Thank You for being my source of power, help, and hope.

psalm-68-closing.verse:
Lord, thank You for being mighty, compassionate, and faithful. You carry my burdens, provide for my needs, defend the vulnerable, and give strength to Your people. Help me walk in worship, trust Your power, and live under Your righteous rule. May my life continually praise You, reflect Your compassion, and declare Your glory to the world.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
psalm-68-closing.prayer:
Lord, thank You for being mighty, compassionate, and faithful. You carry my burdens, provide for my needs, defend the vulnerable, and give strength to Your people. Help me walk in worship, trust Your power, and live under Your righteous rule. May my life continually praise You, reflect Your compassion, and declare Your glory to the world.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
